                               Cover Artist: Doug Chaffee
                          First Publication: Tactical Handbook   CORE CONSTRUCTION RULES
                                        Year: 1994

InMediaRes Productions LLC (order #16148328)
                                                                                                                                          How does a MechWarrior interface with a
                                                                                                                                          BattleMech? How does a ProtoMech really work?
                                                                                                                                          When was the Gauss rifle or the Particle Projector
                                                                                                                                          Cannon developed? Or how can I just make my
                                                                                                                                          own, cool and unique BattleMech design? If
                                                                                                                                          you’ve ever been plagued by these questions,
                                                                                                                                          then get on your coveralls and make sure you’ve
                                                                                                                                          got your astech certification, because you’re
                                                                                                                                          about to dive into BattleTech construction!

                                                                                                                                          A companion volume to Total Warfare™         , the
                                                                                                                                          BattleTech TechManual™ combines all the
                                                                                                                                          construction rules for the various units presented
                                                                                                                                          in that core rules set. Streamlined and updated,
                                                                                                                                          it contains all the core weaponry and equipment
                                                                                                                                          from previous rules set, as well as the newest,
                                                                                                                                          bleeding edge technologies. More than just a
                                                                                                                                          rulebook, however, the TechManual presents
                                                                                                                                          numerous sourcebook-style treatises, providing
                                                                                                                                          the in-universe context for these units and the
                                                                                                                                          technologies of their development. A must have
                                                                                                                                          resource for any BattleTech aficionado!

              The technology base (tech base) of a given unit deter-
            mines its access to components and its general level of
            sophistication. Most units in standard rules (as presented in
            Total Warfare) game play fall into two broad technological
            categories: Clan and Inner Sphere. Clan technology is more
                                                                                         OMNI TECHNOLOGY                                         RECORD SHEETS

                                                                                     The following paragraphs describe a number of basic round-
                                                                                     ing principles that designers will find throughout the various
                                                                                     construction rules in this book.                                    WEAPONS AND
                                                                                                                                                        HEAVY EQUIPMENT
                                                                                     GENERAL ROUNDING (UP, DOWN AND “NORMALLY”)
                                                                                        Recurring rules throughout this book may request that           INFANTRY WEAPONS
                                                                                     a designer “round up,” “round down” or “round normally,”             AND EQUIPMENT
                                                                                     depending on the situation.
                                                                                        Rounding up means to increase the value to the nearest
                                                                                                                                                           COSTS AND
                                                                                     desired number (usually the nearest whole number, though
                                                                                                                                                          AVAILABILITY
                                                                                     some rules ask for the nearest half, the nearest tenth or
                                                                                     even the nearest 5 or more). For example, if a value of 3.1 is
                                                                                     achieved and a designer is asked to “round up to the nearest
                                                                                                                                                          BATTLE VALUE
                                                                                     whole number,” that 3.1 becomes a 4. [Note: Sometimes, the
                                                                                     formula for rounding up may be expressed in text as, “For
                                                                                     every X of something (or fraction thereof ), add Y….” In such
                                                                                     cases, the designer should divide the number of specified                INDEX
                                                                                     items by the value of X and round up to the nearest whole to
                                                                                     find the value of Y.]
                                                                                        Rounding down means to decrease the value to the nearest         RECORD SHEETS
                                                                                     desired number. For example, a value of 3.6 that the designer
                                                                                     must “round down to the nearest whole” becomes a 3. [Note:
                                                                                     As described above with rounding up, sometimes formulas
             DF       DF                                                             that round down are expressed in text as “For every full X of
                                                                                     something, add Y….” In such cases, the designer should divide

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        AVAILABILITY

            PROTOMECH RECORD SHEET
               ProtoMech Record Sheets provide enough data blocks                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       BATTLE VALUE
            for five individual ProtoMechs per page, allowing players to
            track an entire ProtoMech Point at one time. Each ProtoMech
            receives its own overall block of data, which is in turn divided
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            INDEX
            up among smaller sub-blocks as follows.

            ProtoMech Data
               This information, located at the top-left of each ProtoMech                                                                                                                                                                                                                             RECORD SHEETS
            block, is the ProtoMech equivalent of the Unit Data block. It pro-
            vides space to note the ProtoMech’s model name (Type), weight
            (Tons) and Movement Points (Walking/Running/Jumping). Cost
            and BV lines are located below the Pilot Data block.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 RH

                                                                                                                                                                                                 Despite improving relations, a ProtoMech-centered Blood Spirit force raids a Clan Coyote outpost.

            BATTLE ARMOR RECORD SHEET                                                                                                                                                                                                     is designed for use in creating conventional infantry platoons
                Each Battle Armor Record Sheet can record the information                                                                                                                                                                 of the designer’s choosing. The generic sheet is ready-made,
            for five Points or squads of battle armor at a time. Designed to                                                                                                                                                              with data for the basic platoon types already featured in Total
            accommodate any type of battle armor for the Inner Sphere                                                                                                                                                                     Warfare and legal for standard rules play.             or Clans, this sheet—like that for ProtoMechs—is broken into                                                                                                                                                                     The Infantry Record Sheets are arranged along similar lines
            multiple blocks to track entire squads.                                                                                                                                                                                       to those for battle armor, with multiple platoons (Points)
                The data is highly simplified, with each block assigned to                                                                                                                                                                shown per platoon block.             skills of its troopers, Ground MP, Non-Ground MP (including                                                                                                                                                                   trooper icons—each representing a single soldier—makes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          BATTLEMECH

                                               “The Basics of BattleMechs,” Stuttgart School of Defense, Kaumberg, 12 May 3070




InMediaRes Productions LLC (order #16148328)
                Distinguished members of the media, my name is Professor           tural taboos about the BattleMech. For example, in some ‘net
            Dietrich Mathers and I’ve been asked to provide you an                 halls, I would be wise to wear asbestos undies if I dared to
            overview of BattleMechs. As I understand, a number of your             call those shining and magnificent titans of war something so
            editors requested this lecture because Kaumberg’s ‘net has             crass as “giant, armored robots toting huge guns.” So, to strip        INTRODUCTION
            gotten rather toasty towards you. Apparently, legions of               away the cultural baggage that BattleMechs have acquired
            armchair MechWarriors are waging a holy crusade to correct             over the past half-millennium, let me give you this enhanced
            technical inaccuracies in your reporting.                              definition of the BattleMech:                                         CONSTRUCTION
                …I suspect I could have chosen a better phrase than “holy              A BattleMech is an armored combat vehicle.                           BASICS
            crusade,” what with the Blakists’ current misbehavior…At any               That’s right. Don’t be fooled by the sculpted armor and
            rate, the more organized ’Mech-nuts’ petitions must have               lifelike motions of its limbs. A BattleMech is just a tank on         BATTLEMECH
            hurt your ratings, else you wouldn’t be joining me here at the         legs. The arms are complicated turrets, but turrets nonethe-         CONSTRUCTION
            Stuttgart School of Defense.                                           less. The legs are a complicated all-terrain propulsion system.
                To the point, what I hope to achieve today is not to fully         And a BattleMech’s guns and armor do not significantly differ
                                                                                                                                                         INDUSTRIALMECH
            illuminate you regarding all the mysteries of BattleMech               from those found on any other armored combat vehicles in
                                                                                                                                                          CONSTRUCTION
            technology, but rather to explain enough so that when your             use today.
            future reports next involve BattleMechs, you know to ask the               There are, of course, several subtle differences.
            correct questions and get the facts. You should thus—theo-                                                                                     PROTOMECH
            retically—come away from here knowing how to avoid being               INTERNAL STRUCTURE                                                     CONSTRUCTION
            flamed by pimple-faced armchair MechWarriors.                             First and foremost, it is the articulated structure of the legs
                So, this is my lesson plan for the day. First, I’m going to tell   that set ’Mechs (Battle- or otherwise) apart from other vehi-         COMBAT VEHICLE
            you what a BattleMech is. Then I’m going to tell you about their       cles. No other vehicle has articulated legs as capable as those        CONSTRUCTION
            guts, starting at the bones and then covering major compo-             of a ’Mech.
            nents. Finally, I’m going to wrap it all up by explaining how the         Making these legs possible is a chassis, an internal struc-
                                                                                                                                                         SUPPORT VEHICLE
            whole thing gets brought to life. Are we ready? Good.                  ture quite different than those of other vehicles. There is an
                                                                                                                                                          CONSTRUCTION
                                                                                   obvious degree of articulation—joints—not found in the rig-
                                                                                   id bodies of conventional vehicles. And when the first ’Mech
                                                                                   designers sought to harness myomers in the 24th century to            CONV. INFANTRY
                              TERMINOLOGY                                          better animate the limbs of their IndustrialMechs, they found         CONSTRUCTION
                                                                                   the easiest approach was to mimic the layout of humans and
               Now, before I describe the basics, allow me to explain my           other advanced animals: an endoskeleton.                               BATTLE ARMOR
            terminology. First, when I say “BattleMech,” I’m referring to             Having an endo-skeletal frame means that many of a                  CONSTRUCTION
            BattleMechs and OmniMechs. If I have something to say about            ’Mech’s systems sit on the exterior of the frame rather than
            Omnis alone, I will say “OmniMech.” But if I just say “’Mech,” I’m     being caged within a frame, roughly similar to the way your
            referring to any type of ’Mech, including IndustrialMechs.                                                                                   AEROSPACE UNIT
                                                                                   skeleton serves as an internal support for muscles and organs.
               Also, I believe some of you got in trouble from the ’Mech-                                                                                 CONSTRUCTION
                                                                                   As rigid and unyielding as BattleMech armor might seem, few
            heads for taking your biological analogies to ’Mechs too liter-        ’Mech designs use the armor to assist the internal structure’s
            ally. Before I begin confusing you, I’m going to warn you right        load-bearing duty. BattleMech armor is too thin for its width         WEAPONS AND
            now that while I will be using terms like “bones” and “joints”         and height to be anything but a wobbly sheet.                        HEAVY EQUIPMENT
            and “muscles,” keep in mind I am talking about mechanical                 Wait, “wobbly” was another bad word choice, wasn’t it?
            systems. For example, the “bones” of a BattleMech’s chassis
                                                                                                                                                        INFANTRY WEAPONS
            are composite structural components that can endure mega-              The Frame                                                              AND EQUIPMENT
            newton loads while supporting an exterior armor shell, dif-               So, let’s start talking about internal structure components
            fusing excess electrical currents from PPC strikes, carrying a         with the bones. A ’Mech typically only has about sixteen to
            dense web of data and power lines, and anchoring myomer                                                                                        COSTS AND
                                                                                   twenty-five “bones.” This is partly because some structures
            bundles. As a result, they appear no more like human bones                                                                                    AVAILABILITY
                                                                                   that might be assembled from a dozen or more bones in a
            than the wings of a large aircraft resemble a bird’s wings.            human, like the ribcage, are a one-piece cage in ’Mechs. In
                                                                                   other areas ’Mechs simply don’t need the complexity of the             BATTLE VALUE
                                                                                   human model, like the foot, where shock pads are used. One
             BATTLEMECHS: THE BASICS                                               end result of this structural streamlining compared to the hu-
                                                                                   man inspiration is that ’Mechs generally have less flexibility
                                                                                                                                                              INDEX
               You would have to be a blind, deaf, and dumb inhabitant             and articulation than their organic creators.
            of a Deep Periphery lost colony to not know basically what                The ’Mech’s so-called bones differ between ’Mech classes.
            a BattleMech is. But just in case, a BattleMech is a giant, ar-        The cheap, heavy bones of IndustrialMechs, for instance, are
            mored robot toting huge guns, piloted by those modern                  quite different from BattleMech bones and are beyond the              RECORD SHEETS
            knights called MechWarriors.                                           scope of this discussion. The bones of BattleMechs, mean-
               That is a simplification, of course. As many of you have            while, fall into two major designs at the moment: standard
            learned, no doubt, there is a little more to these machines            structure and endo-steel structure.
            than that.                                                                Standard BattleMech structure uses major components
               Your recent problems originate not only from people                 that are composites of foamed aluminum—
            shouting at you for technical errors, but also for violating cul-         Eh? Yes? Could you speak up a bit for the microph—?


                                                                                                                                                               31
InMediaRes Productions LLC (order #16148328)
               …Thank you. No, when I say “composite,” I mean it in the cor-            it runs into buckling problems unless you make the bones wider.
            rect engineering sense of “a material made of several distinct              That’s how cardboard turns thin sheets of paper into a stiff struc-
            components.” I am not referring to the experimental and fragile             ture: it makes the structure thicker with the corrugated paper be-
            “composite internal structure” that you’ve heard of from Solaris            tween two outer sheets. Endo-steel uses a larger foam core inside
            Tech Monthly. I’m not going to cover experimental stuff like that           the thinner shell.
            today. Hopefully after we’re done here, you’ll be able to investi-              Now, because of its composition, endo-steel is produced in
            gate those topics on your own.                                              zero-G to avoid chemical segregation. Er, that is, some of the al-
               Anyway, the major components—the major “bones” of the                    loying agents want to separate like oil and water and thus make
            standard internal structure—are composite structures with a                 the steel brittle and weak, but they’ll stay mixed in zero-G until
            core of ultra-light foamed aluminum, shrouded in carefully ori-             solidification. Structural designers also make endo-steel’s foam
            ented wraps of silicon carbide fibers. This assembly is then clad           core in zero-G, where foams form a more regular pore size and
            with titanium-alloyed steel. The fiber layer also has assorted              thus have superior strength. Zero-G processing makes endo-
            structural sensors and data lines woven into it, while the exterior         steel expensive, but the elimination of the fiber layer allows it to
            is rigged to mount assorted equipment. Struts extend outward                be produced faster than standard structures.
            from the bones to support the armor shell without interfering                   So, those are the bones of ’Mechs. Next: the joints that string
            with the myomer rigging. Weapons frame attachments are cus-                 them together.
            tom-designed for each ’Mech.
                                                                                        Actuators
            Endo-Steel                                                                     A ’Mech’s joints are generally referred to as its actuators. This
               Endo-steel internal structures are basically the same as standard        includes more than just the hinges between bones. An actuator
            structures in layout, but differ in materials. Endo-steel structures uti-   includes the joint itself, the associated myomer bundles and the
            lize endomorphic steel (hence their name). This endo-steel is much          motor control units.
            stronger than the steel of standard BattleMech frames, which allows            The actual joint is typically a ball joint like your hip or a hinge
            their structural walls to be thinner and lighter for the same strength.     like your elbow, and it is the simplest component of an actuator.
            The thinner walls would make the bones less stiff for the same diam-        The joints are sealed and are usually filled with dry lubricants
            eter of bone, so endo-steel bones have to be noticeably larger.             like graphite.
               Yes? I can hear you— …No. Stiffness and strength aren’t the                 The joints are moved by the myomers, just as your joints are
            same qualities. A thick cardboard panel is stiffer, less prone to           moved by their associated muscles. Before I get to the myomers,
            buckling, than a thin sheet of metal even though the metal is               however, I want to describe how the myomers and joints are con-
            much stronger. Endo-steel is stronger, but because it is thinner,           trolled. Each joint has an associated Motor Control Unit (MCU,
                                                                                        for short) that sends commands—in the form of electrical pow-
                                                                                        er—to the myomer bundles. The MCUs are local managers that
                                                                                        organize thousands of myomer fibers in each myomer bundle,
                                                                                        contracting them and monitoring feedback. These MCUs are in
                                                                                        turn commanded by higher-order control systems that interpret
                                                                                        the MechWarrior’s input for the benefit of these units.

                                                                                        Myomers
                                                                                           A ’Mech’s muscles—called myomers—are made up of bundles
                                                                                        of microscopically thin plastic tubes filled with a contracting sub-
                                                                                        stance. Each tube, basically made of polyacetylene, is individually
                                                                                        extruded in microscopically thin forms and spun into the bundle.
                                                                                        The contractile filling, called acti-strandular fiber, is crapped out
                                                                                        by vats of genetically engineered bacteria, rather like how alco-
                                                                                        hol production occurs. This acti-strandular precursor material,
                                                                                        strained out of the vats, is mixed with specific polymers, and then
                                                                                        squirted into the tubes. The polyacetylene tubes are then electri-
                                                                                        fied and the acti-strandular precursor material arranges itself into
                                                                                        intricate, complex nanoscale structures akin to the contractile
                                                                                        protein filaments in natural muscle (myosin and actin filaments).
                                                                                           When the acti-strandular fibers are stimulated by sufficient
                                                                                        electrical energy, the fibers contract sharply. The process is vir-
                                                                                        tually identical to the contraction of protein filaments in natu-
                                                                                        ral muscles, but with an electrical power source, rather than a
                                                                                        chemical one. Also like natural muscle, the contraction is an all-
                                                                                        or-nothing process. The level of strength generated by myomer
                                                                                        bundles is regulated by the number of myomer fibers triggered,
                                                                                        rather than the amount of current itself. As myomers are much
                                                                                        more powerful by weight than natural muscle, and can be built
                                                                                        on larger scales, it is their use that makes efficient ’Mechs pos-




InMediaRes Productions LLC (order #16148328)
                                                                                   ARMOR
                                                                                     Armor is the last of the “dumb” components in a BattleMech
                                                                                   before I get to the juicy stuff.
                                                                                                                                                           INTRODUCTION
                                                                                   The Layers
                                                                                      Standard BattleMech armor consists of multiple layers.
                                                                                   Only two of these layers are armor in the truest sense. The            CONSTRUCTION
                                                                                   other two layers play supporting roles.                                   BASICS
                                                                                      The outer layer of armor is an extremely strong, extremely
                                                                                   hard iron alloy…a steel. It is intended to fragment projec-            BATTLEMECH
                                                                                   tiles and/or—hey, have you heard anyone use “and/or” aloud            CONSTRUCTION
                                                                                   before?—to fragment projectiles and/or ablate protectively
                                                                                   in the face of energy attacks. The grains of this steel, that is,      INDUSTRIALMECH
                                                                                   its crystals, are carefully aligned for maximum strength and            CONSTRUCTION
                                                                                   radiation-treated to further hardness and strength. Though
                                                                                   phenomenally strong and hard, the steel pays for these prop-
                                                                                   erties by being quite brittle. In fact, the steel is brittle enough      PROTOMECH
                                                                                   that the second layer of armor that backs the steel is a ce-            CONSTRUCTION
                                                                                   ramic, cubic boron nitride.
                                                                                      The point of the boron nitride layer is to act as a catcher’s       COMBAT VEHICLE
                                                                                   mitt for the fragments and plasma of the steel. Another very            CONSTRUCTION
                                                                                   strong, very hard material with a brittleness problem, boron
                                                                                   nitride is carefully processed to avoid any porosity and incor-        SUPPORT VEHICLE
                                                                                   porates an additional web of artificial diamond fibers to make          CONSTRUCTION
                                                                                   the ceramic a fine backstop for the steel outer layer.
                                                                                      Now, during the Succession Wars, some armor manufactur-
                                                                                   ers lost the fiber-making techniques and resorted to diamond           CONV. INFANTRY
                                                                                   powders, which could be made from natural diamonds. This               CONSTRUCTION

                                                                                                                                                         HEAVY EQUIPMENT
            bundles in a ’Mech’s fingers are multi-kilowatt motors, while

               And while we’re talking about myomers as electrical mo-                                                                                      COSTS AND
            tors, I’d like to take a moment to discount a theory being                                                                                     AVAILABILITY
            kicked around by—was it Lasers and Slugs magazine?—that
            an easy way to cripple ’Mechs would be to hit them with riot
            control stun bullets. Sorry, no. A little jolt of electricity from a                                                                           BATTLE VALUE
            bullet isn’t going to make a ’Mech’s myomers twitch anymore
            than a flashlight battery will budge the multi-megawatt mo-
            tors of a fusion-electric freight train.                                                                                                           INDEX
               On a related note, there’s the misconception that lightning
            and PPCs (which actually are nothing like lightning) should
            spasm a ’Mech and rip its myomers apart. ’Mechs protect
            themselves from these electrical dangers much like your                                                                                       RECORD SHEETS
            home does with lightning rods. ’Mech armor and skeletons
            are designed to provide low-resistance routes for stray elec-
            tricity to ground out safely without ever going through the
            myomers. And once the armor’s gone…well, once the armor’s
            gone, you’re shooting PPCs into bundles of plastic. Damage
            will be done one way or another.

            Containment and Power Generation
                                                                                                                                                                     INDEX
              So, you’ve got this super-hot ball of hydrogen plasma be-
            ing turned into helium. What keeps it from melting the en-
            gine? Magnetic fields. Plasma is electrically charged, so it can
            be pushed around by magnetic fields. There are fields in the                                                                                        RECORD SHEETS
            plasma itself and fields generated outside the plasma. The
            plasma doesn’t touch the wall. In fact, outside the plasma, the
            reactor chamber is a vacuum for insulation.
              How does the power come out of the plasma? Two ways.
              The first way is a tongue twister called “magnetohydrody-
            namics,” MHD for short. The short and semi-correct description

            Controls
               Configurability aside, the controls for a machine as compli-
            cated as a BattleMech are actually surprisingly simple. And I’ll
            say it now: it’s not because the MechWarrior links directly with
            the BattleMech through the neurohelmet. Leave that notion to
            the cheap holovids. No, this simplicity is because the BattleMech
            itself does most of the work. I’ll explain that when I tie all these
            components together.
               BattleMechs typically have two or three major hand controls, con-
            trol sticks. One control stick is the throttle, typically on the left side
            of the cockpit. The other is the main targeting control stick that aims
            the ’Mech’s weapons. Yes, despite all the arms and adjustable guns
            on a ’Mech, you just point and click to fire. Some BattleMechs will
            add a second, left-hand weapon control stick to simultaneously aim
            different groups of weapons, but this rarely works well in practice.
               Steering is accomplished with a pair of foot pedals. You press
            the right pedal, the ’Mech turns right. The foot pedals also control
            jump jets if the ’Mech has them. Hit both pedals at once, the jump
            jets trigger; mid-air steering is usually accomplished with the
            pedals, but complicated aerial maneuvering may be performed
            with the regular hand controls. Crosshairs can pick a landing spot
            as well as target a foe.
               When hand actuators are present on a ’Mech, most of their
            actions require little input from the MechWarrior. As I’ll describe
            later, ’Mechs generally have enough intelligence to recognize a
            simple “grab command” as aimed by a control stick and cross-
            hairs, and can thus pick up improvised clubs or cargo without
            detailed input from the MechWarrior. Punching is trivial: click the
            punch mode switch, aim the crosshairs, and pull the punch trig-
            ger. Ditto for using clubs and hatchets. For fine hand manipula-
            tions, sensors built into the gloves of MechWarriors or separate
            waldo gloves can allow a ’Mech to mimic the gestures of its
            MechWarriors, at least when the glove sensors are activated.
               Of course, BattleMechs can do more than just turn left or right,
            or move backwards and forwards. Talented MechWarriors have
            gotten assault ’Mechs to skip sideways to avoid missiles, executed
            handstands under carefully controlled conditions, and otherwise
            tapped some of the often-unused potential of a BattleMech’s
            limbs for complicated movements. For now, you’re just getting
            the two-kroner overview.
               More complicated movements involve more complicated
            combinations of controls. The steering pedals don’t just push
            back and forth. They can also tilt and twist. Throttle control levers
            and fire control can also provide steering and movement input.
            And while neurohelmets primarily serve to correct balance, they
            can help clarify the MechWarrior’s intent to the BattleMech.
               Now, you’ll notice in these pictures multitudes of other switch-
            es, but those are the nitty-gritty details of ’Mech operation: comm
            systems, ECM, missile alerts, ammo displays, navigation controls,
            diagnostics, environmental controls and so forth. These systems
            aid the MechWarrior in a host of secondary operations and com-
            bat at the touch of a button or the quick scan of a readout.

            Displays

            incorporated some displays. This is particularly true of the cruder

                                                                                                                                                                  INDEX
               The Critical Space Table presumes a default IndustrialMech
            configuration, built using standard engines, gyros, cockpit sys-
            tems and—in the case of humanoid chassis—two complete
            sets of arm and hand actuators. The removal of hand and/or                                                                                        RECORD SHEETS
            lower arm actuators on one or both arms can increase the
            amount of open space overall at the designer’s option, though
            doing so can impair the IndustrialMech’s ability to function
            well under certain conditions. Designers should carefully con-                                                                         ??LS
            sider the effects of exercising such “space-saving” measures.

            the most basic aspects of the IndustrialMech’s design, determin-
            ing what kind of ’Mech it is, its technology base, weight class and                Edward’s Buster is intended to

                                                                                             medium weight of 50 tons.

                                SMSL

                                            After a round of training exercises, this Blood Spirit ProtoMech point is sent in for a routine maintenance check.

               A uniquely Clan concept (the Inner Sphere has yet to mimic                               Tech Rating
            the technology), ProtoMechs (sometimes referred to simply as                                   In the Weapons and Equipment Tables used for creating Proto-
            “Protos”) occupy a middle ground between heavy battle armor                                 Mechs and other units in this book, all items receive a Technology
            and true BattleMechs in BattleTech. Intended as a frugal answer                             Rating in addition to a technology base. This rating (expressed as
            to BattleMechs for more resource-poor Clans, ProtoMechs are                                 a series of letter grades that define the level of technology, and its
            much smaller than BattleMechs, and most are much tougher than                               availability through the major eras of BattleTech history) helps to
            battlesuits. Operating in groups, ProtoMechs can be a powerful                              define the item’s level of sophistication and may be used to help
            weapon in battle.                                                                           standardize the level of “advancement” the unit demonstrates
               The following system enables players to construct unique                                 for purposes of era-based campaigning. Beyond this, however,
            ProtoMechs for BattleTech games that conform to the standard                                an item’s Technology Rating has no direct bearing on ProtoMech
            rules presented in Total Warfare. As these rules can be fairly math-                        design. See Costs and Availability (p. 274) for more on Tech Rating.
            intensive, ProtoMech designers are encouraged to do all work
            on scratch paper before committing the resulting design’s final                             OmniMechs
            specifications to an appropriate blank record sheet.                                         ProtoMechs may not be constructed as OmniMechs.

                                                                                                        WEIGHT

                        PROTOMECH BASICS                                                                   ProtoMechs use a kilogram standard, with all units legal for
                                                                                                        Total Warfare standard play weighing between 2,000 and 9,000
                                                                                                        kilograms, increasing in increments of 1,000 kilograms. Because
              Though ProtoMechs are thematically based on BattleMech                                    many items that may be mounted on Protos express their weight
            design, their construction is slightly more simplified. Only avail-                         in tonnage, these items must be converted to the kilogram stan-
            able to a Clan Technology Base, their lighter weight and simpler                            dard by multiplying their tonnage values by 1,000.
            space system make for a shorter process. These factors, broadly                                At the end of ProtoMech construction, a Proto’s total mass is
            overviewed in the Construction Basics section (see p. 44), are                              expressed in terms of tonnage on its record sheet for simplicity.
            expanded on below.                                                                          For example, a 6,000-kilogram ProtoMech is listed on its record
                                                                                                        sheet as a 6-ton design.
            TECHNOLOGY BASE
               Under these rules, ProtoMechs may only be constructed using                              Weight Classes
            a Clan Technology Base. Any weapons and equipment carried by                                  ProtoMechs loosely fall into four main weight classes, deter-
            a ProtoMech must be selected from those available to the Clans.                             mined by their tonnage. Most designs in these classes share
                                                                                                        similar features, though the lighter weight of ProtoMechs (com-
                                                                                                        pared to BattleMechs) often blurs their distinctions.

               ProtoMechs vent heat the same way conventional vehicles do,            Unlike most other units, ProtoMechs mount their armor (an
            and so do not require heat sinks for movement or the use of non-       advanced composite incompatible with non-ProtoMech units)
            energy weapons. Any ProtoMech that mounts energy weapons,              on a point-by-point basis, rather than in half- or full-ton lots. Each
            however, must install enough sinks to dissipate the heat gener-        armor point mounted on a ProtoMech weighs 50 kilograms and
            ated by all such weapons. A ProtoMech that carries an ER medium        must be allocated to one of the Proto’s five locations (or six, if it
            laser, for example, must also carry 5 heat sinks to handle the heat.   carries a main gun). Remember that both legs count as a single
            Because this step depends more heavily on the weapons that will        location, and note that no ProtoMech location has a front/rear
            be installed, the designer may hold off assigning heat sinks until     facing, as BattleMechs do.

                                                                                             All Combat Vehicles constructed under these rules may carry
                                                                                          only 1 turret, except for VTOLs, which may not carry turrets.
                                                                                             Rotors: All VTOLs automatically receive a Rotor location, and
                                                                                          must allocate internal structure boxes to that location as well.
                                                                                          Unlike the turret for other Combat Vehicle types, rotors are not
                                                                                          optional for VTOLs, nor may any equipment or weapons be
                                                                                          mounted in the Rotor location.
                                                                                             Tractor/Trailer Vehicles: At the designer’s option, Combat
                                                                                          Vehicles with a Tracked or Wheeled motive type may be built
                                                                                          as Tractor or Trailer Units. These units must include a hitching
                                                                                          mechanism into their frames at no cost in weight, but 1 item slot
                                                                                          per hitch. Hitches may be place in the front or rear locations (or
                                                                                          even both) for both Tractor and Trailer units (Tractors with a hitch

              STEP 2: INSTALL ENGINES AND                                                               To compute a Combat Vehicle’s Engine Rating, the designer
                                                                                                      simply multiplies the vehicle’s tonnage by its desired Cruising
                                                                                                      MP, then subtracts from this result the suspension factor appro-

                   CONTROL SYSTEMS                                                                    priate to the vehicle’s tonnage and motive type. A vehicle’s Flank
                                                                                                      MP is also calculated at this time; multiply the Cruising MP by 1.5
                                                                                                      and round up to the nearest whole number. The Engine Rating
              The second step in Combat Vehicle design is the installation of                         and Mobility Formulas Table below provides the formulas for
            the vehicle’s engines and control systems. This step establishes the                      computing Combat Vehicle Engine Ratings and also provides
            key factors in the Combat Vehicle’s mobility, including the size and                      the suspension factors for various Combat Vehicle motive types
            performance of its engine, the mass of its lift/dive systems (if any)                     and weights.
            and the weight of the control systems the crew uses during battle.                          Under these rules, no Combat Vehicle may be constructed with
                                                                                                      an Engine Rating below 10 or above 400. An engine may never

                                                      ENGINE RATING AND MOBILITY FORMULAS
                                              Base Engine Rating = Vehicle Tonnage x Desired Cruising MP
                                              Final Engine Rating = Base Engine Rating – Suspension Factor*
                                              Flank MP = Desired Cruising MP x 1.5 (round up)

               *Minimum Final Rating = 10; Maximum Final Rating = 400; Round up results to the nearest multiple of 5.

                  Combat Vehicle Suspension Factors

                     (31-40 tons)                           175                                       (All)                                       0
                     (41-50 tons)                           235                                    VTOL

               OmniVehicles: OmniVehicles of Clan or Inner Sphere design                    Heat sinks take up no item space in Combat Vehicle design,
            may add more heat sinks at this stage, or—at the designer’s                  so Rick moves on to the next step.
            option—may stick with whatever weight-free sinks they may
            receive from their fusion engines. Additional heat sinks for later
            alternate configurations may be mounted in Omni-pods like
            other weapons and equipment (at a cost in weight, but not item
            space). Such additional sinks may then be easily determined by
            the vehicle’s configuration, based on its chassis and the total heat
            of all energy weapons on that configuration.

                Tech Rating     Technology Level                                                   Sample Unit Types
                A               Primitive (19th to early 20th centuries)                           Wheeled Support Vehicles and Small Airships
                B               Low-Tech (late 20th century)                                       Tracked Support Vehicles and Fixed-Wing Support Vehicles
                C               Common Tech (21st to 22nd centuries)                               Hovercraft Support Vehicles and VTOL Support Vehicles
                D               High-Tech (Age of War and Succession Wars)                         Combat Vehicles and BattleMechs
                E               Advanced (Star League/Post-3050 Inner Sphere)                      Inner Sphere OmniMechs and OmniVehicles
                F               Hyper-Advanced (Clan/Post-3065 Inner Sphere)                       Clan OmniMechs and OmniVehicles

                Chassis Modification                Min. Tech Rating         Multiplier    Modification Restrictions
                Amphibious                                  C                   1.75       Not available to Hover or Naval Support Vehicles
                Armored*                                    A                    1.5       Not available to Airship Support Vehicles
                Bicycle**                                   A                   0.75       Small Wheeled and Small Hover Support Vehicles Only
                Convertible                                 A                    1.1       Wheeled, Tracked and Hover Support Vehicles Only
                Dune Buggy†                                 B                    1.5       Wheeled Support Vehicles Only
                Environmental Sealing                       B                     2        No Restrictions
                Hydrofoil                                   B                    1.7       Naval Support Vehicles (up to 100 tons in Weight) Only
                Monocycle**                                 B                    0.5       Small Wheeled and Small Hover Support Vehicles Only
                Off-Road                                    B                    1.5       Wheeled Support Vehicles Only
                Omni                                        E                     1        No Restrictions
                Prop                                        B                    1.2       Fixed-Wing Support Vehicles Only
                Snowmobile†                                 B                   1.75       Wheeled and Tracked Support Vehicles Only
                STOL                                        C                    1.5       Fixed-Wing Support Vehicles Only
                Submersible                                 B                    1.8       Naval Support Vehicles Only
                Tractor (Wheel/Track)                       A                    1.2       Wheeled/Tracked Support Vehicles Only
                Tractor (Naval)                             A                    1.2       Naval Support Vehicles Only
                Trailer                                     A                    0.8       Wheeled and Tracked Support Vehicles Only
                Ultra-Light*                                D                    0.5       Small Support Vehicles Only
                VSTOL                                       C                     2        Fixed-Wing Motive Types Only

               *Armored and Ultra-Light modifications may not be combined.
               **Bicycle and Monocycle modifications may not be combined.
               †Snowmobile and Dune Buggy modifications may not be combined with each other or with Amphibious and/or Off-Road modifications.

                                                       CHASSIS MODIFICATION DESCRIPTIONS
                                                                                                                                                                     INTRODUCTION
                Modification                                                                      Description/Features
                Amphibious                          May traverse or land on water of any depth (unit is considered to be afloat on the water’s surface)
                                                                                                                                                                     CONSTRUCTION
                Armored                             May mount heavier armor types (marked with an asterisk on the Support Vehicle Armor Table)                          BASICS
                Bicycle                             May enter Light Woods terrain, if otherwise restricted from doing so
                Convertible                         May not mount a turret; Unit receives a Crew Killed result if hit in a Turret location while top is open          BATTLEMECH
                                                                                                                                                                     CONSTRUCTION
                Dune Buggy                          Operates normally only on Sand; When not on Sand: –1 Cruising MP, and unit may not use Flank MP*
                Environmental Sealing               Fission-, Fusion- and Electric-engine units may operate normally in vacuum or underwater unless breached        INDUSTRIALMECH
                Hydrofoil                           May operate as a hydrofoil; multiply base Cruising MP by 1.25 (round normally, rounding .5 up)                   CONSTRUCTION

                Monocycle                           May enter Light Woods terrain, if otherwise restricted from doing so
                                                                                                                                                                      PROTOMECH
                Off-Road                            Operates normally on and off Paved terrain; Unit still considered immobile if it enters prohibited terrain       CONSTRUCTION
                Omni                                May be constructed as an OmniVehicle (but may not use Mechanized Infantry rules)
                                                    Cannot operate above atmospheric row 1; Only moves 1 hex per turn on the high-altitude map;                      COMBAT VEHICLE
                Prop
                                                    Goes Out of Control at Velocity 8+                                                                                CONSTRUCTION
                Snowmobile                          Operates normally only on Snow/Ice; When not on Snow or Ice: –1 Cruising MP, and may not use Flank MP*
                STOL                                May land and takeoff with only 300 meters (10 hexes) of runway space                                           SUPPORT VEHICLE
                                                                                                                                                                    CONSTRUCTION
                Submersible                         May operate as a submarine (may not use hydrofoil mod, if equipped, when submerged)
                Tractor (Wheel/Track)               May tow other ground units over non-water terrain (up to towing unit’s mass)                                     CONV. INFANTRY
                Tractor (Naval)                     May tow other naval units over water (up to 5x the towing unit’s mass)                                           CONSTRUCTION
                Trailer                             No engine required (unit may be towed by a suitable Tractor); Not required for naval units
                Ultra-Light                         May not mount armor better than a BAR of 5 due to lighter structure design                                       BATTLE ARMOR
                                                                                                                                                                     CONSTRUCTION
                VSTOL                               May operate as an STOL or may take off and land vertically

               * No effect in standard rules play                                                                                                                    AEROSPACE UNIT

                                                                                                                    engines of Tech Rating C, Fission engines of all Tech Ratings, and
                                                                                                                    all hover-based Support Vehicles have minimum engine weights

                   CONTROL SYSTEMS                                                                                  that must be satisfied during construction. Fusion Engines with
                                                                                                                    a Tech Rating of D or higher have a minimum engine weight of
                                                                                                                    0.25 tons (250 kilograms). For Fission engines (and Fusion engines
               The second step in Support Vehicle design is the installation of                                     of Tech Rating C), this minimum engine weight is 5 tons (5,000
            the vehicle’s engines and control systems. This step establishes                                        kilograms), regardless of the Support Vehicle’s size, weight, speed

            size and performance of its engine and the weight of the control                                        this minimum weight is equal to 20 percent of the vehicle’s total
            systems the crew uses during operations.                                                                weight, rounded up to the nearest kilogram for Small vehicles, or
                                                                                                                    to the nearest half-ton for hovercraft 5 tons and over. If both of

              This is my diary.                                                   certainly didn’t make lugging around a ten-kilogram backpack
              I record whatever I like, whenever I like, HOW I LIKE.              terribly recreational. Adding to the fun, each of them carried a
              I’m here because I have to be.                                      meter-long, six-kilogram rifle, which tended to get caught in         INTRODUCTION

                                                                                  on two legs. A stuffed turtle with a cooking-pot on its head—        CONSTRUCTION
            3 October 3068                                                            “Cover!”                                                            BASICS
              Oh man, I thought I’d have more time!                                   Sighing silently, Stefan Palmer let himself drop flat behind
                                                                                  the nearest tree stump at his group leader’s hushed call.              BATTLEMECH

                                                                                                                                                            INDEX

            CHOOSE TECHNOLOGY BASE (AFFILIATION) AND SIZE
              The technology base for an infantry platoon determines what
            weapons and equipment are available to its troopers, as well as
            their overall size and organization. Clan conventional infantry
            platoons (called Points) may only use items from the Infantry
            Platoon Equipment Table that are available to the Clans, while
            Inner Sphere (and Periphery) platoons may only use technology
            available to the Inner Sphere.

            Affiliation and Infantry Deployment
               In general, Clan conventional infantry operates on a strict
            base-5 squad system, where 5 troopers make up a squad and
            5 squads make up a Point of infantry with few variations. Inner
            Sphere platoons, by comparison, typically follow a base-7 squad
            system, with greater variations especially among self-mechanized
            conventional platoons.
               Many of the variations in platoon sizes and arrangements come
            from the faction (affiliation) fielding the infantry. While the infan-
            try types shown in Total Warfare represent the standards used
                                                                                     RARA
            by the majority of the Inner Sphere, some factions—particularly
                                                                                      An unsuspecting Word of Blake Black Knight is stalked by infantry hidden in the nearby ruins.

                Class = Small or Medium                                                    No Effect                                             Not Applicable

                     Damage Type             Game Play Effect

                           (E)               Energy Weapon: Attacks as standard infantry ranged weapon

                           (P)               Point-Blank Weapon: +1 to-hit at 0 Hexes; May not be used by mechanized infantry types

                 will be a basic laser rifle unit. The standard laser rifle is        squads in the platoon receive 1 Secondary Weapon of the same

                 laser rifle is given Damage Type (E), indicating an energy
                 weapon used for standard ranged attacks, and has no
                 special abilities. The laser rifle has a Base Range of 2, which
                 will give the platoon a –2 to-hit modifier at a range of 0
                 hexes (the platoon’s same hex), no modifier at a range of 1
                 to 2 hexes, a +2 modifier at ranges of 3 to 4 hexes, and a +4
                 to-hit modifier at 5 to 6 hexes. The laser rifle also provides
                 each trooper with a Damage Value of 0.28. Finally, a Crew
                 Value of 1 means that the use of laser rifles will not affect
                 the platoon’s to-hit numbers, and that each trooper in the
                 platoon may carry one.

                    For his Marian foot infantry platoon, Eberhard chooses
                 standard ballistic rifles as a Primary Weapon. From the
                 Infantry Weapons List, he chooses the basic auto-rifle,
                 which is listed as a Standard (B) weapon, for standard
                 ranged attack capability with a Base Range of 1, and a
                 Damage Value of 0.52 points per trooper. The Crew Value
                 of 1 means that the auto-rifles function without added
                 modifiers at close range and may be carried by all troop-
                 ers. Eberhard notes that the Base Range will yield a –2
                 to-hit modifier at 0 hexes/same-hex range, no modifier at
                 1 hex, +2 at 2 hexes and +4 at 3 hexes.

                 by 20, Jason finds that the average damage per trooper for
                 his Point is 0.85 (17 Damage ÷ 20 troops = 0.85 Damage per                             PLATOON NOMENCLATURE
                 trooper). Multiplying each trooper individually, he determines                            Though designers may choose any name desired for their
                 the Damage Value of the platoon for troopers numbered 1                                infantry platoons (as with all other units), as a rule of thumb, most
                 through 20.                                                                            conventional infantry units in BattleTech are identified by their
                                                                                                        motive and weapon types for ease of reference. For instance, a
                                                                                                        platoon equipped with auto-rifles that moves by jumping might
                                                                                                        be called “Jump Rifle Infantry” or “Rifle Infantry (Jump)”.
                                                                                                           If such platoons are also equipped with Secondary Weapons
                                                                                                        that enhance their abilities, these Secondary Weapons may be
                                                                                                        used to differentiate the platoon instead; for example, a platoon
                                                                                                        armed with auto-rifles backed up by SRMs may call itself an
                                                                                                        SRM platoon. Because the choice of weapon and motive types
                                                                                                        dominates the platoon’s function so completely and can easily
                                                                                                        affect such naming decisions, it is generally in this final step that a
                                                                                                        designer can best determine the proper nomenclature for a new
                                                                                                        conventional infantry platoon.

                                                                                                        INFANTRY AND CARGO TRANSPORTATION
                                                                                                           When constructing infantry using these rules, the designer may
                                                                                                        gain insight into how many troopers may be contained aboard a
                                                                                                        vehicle with a cargo capacity measured in tonnage. The capacity
                                                                                                        needed by an infantry platoon type is listed in the infantry bay
                                                                                                        rules for such vehicles, and the weights generated by these rules
                                                                                                        are intended to match them. When in doubt, however, use the
                                                                                                        weight of the platoon versus the weight assigned to cargo by
                                                                                                        the transporting vehicle(s) as a guide to determining how many
                                                                                                        troops may be placed aboard a transport. While the result may
                                                                                                        force the player to break up a platoon into groups smaller than
                                                                                                        its component squads, it is not uncommon for a platoon to be
                                                                                                        carried into battle in such a fashion aboard multiple APCs.
                                                                                                           Oversized platoons—those that take up multiple lines on the
                                                                                                        Infantry Record Sheet—should divide this total in proportion to
             RARA                                                                                      the troopers on each line, and record the weight for each line of
                                                                                                        troops separately. Doing so enables the platoon to better account
                 The swarming infantry converge on the BattleMech’s cockpit, banking on a quick-kill.   for its component sub-platoons.

                                        BATTLE ARMOR BASICS
               [Per your request, Dr. Parker has asked me to pull together some basic background on battle armor. The following is a transcript of an
            induction and orientation briefing typical of the kind given to new candidates in the NAIS Accelerated Battle Armor Training (ABAT) program.
            Presented in Sergeant Major Zeb Isacs Macintyre’s own irrepressible style, it nonetheless serves as an informative discourse on the fundamen-
            tals of battle armor technology and its application on the modern battlefield. —J.S.]
